title: Regular Assessment of Licensed Manning Agencies
content:
SECTION 36. Scope of Regular Assessment. - The Assessment is required for upgrading provisional licenses to regular licenses, renewal of licenses and branch authority, transfer of office, and biennially after license renewal. It covers: (a) maintenance of minimum office space, facilities, and equipment; (b) maintenance of required signages and postings including POEA door sticker, agency license, organizational chart, 'Kaukulang Bayad' poster, and anti-illegal recruitment campaign posters; (c) examination of books of accounts, official receipts, and financial statements; and (d) compliance with general labor standards and occupational safety and health standards.

SECTION 37. Issuance of Authority to Assess. - The LRO Director or authorized representative issues authority to assess to duly authorized LLCOs, specifying names, licensed manning agency, purpose, and validity. The LRO Director or authorized representative may request the DOLE Regional Director to conduct assessments including branches.

SECTION 38. Conduct and Report of Assessment. - LLCO presents authority to the licensed manning agency; a responsible agency representative and a rank-and-file employee representative must be present during assessment. Assessment follows the checklist in Annex 'A'.

SECTION 39. Issuance of Notice of Results. - LLCO issues Notice of Results indicating compliance or deficiencies with POEA rules, labor laws, and social legislation; agency representative acknowledges receipt.

SECTION 40. Report of Assessment. - LLCO endorses assessment results related to overseas employment to LRO Director, or to DOLE Regional Director for general labor and occupational safety and health standards.

SECTION 41. Issuance of Certificate of Compliance. - Agencies complying with overseas employment rules receive a Certificate of Compliance valid for two years from POEA Administrator within five days of compliance. Certificates for general labor and occupational safety and health standards are issued by the DOLE Regional Director. Certificates cover branch offices included in license renewal.

SECTION 42. Compliance with Deficiencies. - Upon non-compliance, LLCO issues Notice of Results with assessment checklist indicating deficiencies. Agencies must comply as follows: (a) correct office facilities, equipment, postings, and signages deficiencies within five working days; failure results in suspension of documentary processing until compliance; (b) submit proof of maintaining minimum office space within 30 days; failure results in suspension of documentary processing until compliance; (c) if audited financial statement shows capitalization below required minimum, infuse additional capital within 30 days and submit SEC certificate or shareholder cash advances; failure results in license suspension until compliance; (d) present books of accounts and official receipts within 10 days or face suspension of documentary processing; (e) unauthorized personnel found on premises reported for administrative proceedings. DOLE has jurisdiction over compliance with general labor and occupational safety and health standards per LLCS Rules.
